There are supposed to be a small stand of American Persimmon on this block. I will tag the exact spot this upcoming fall after getting a bunch!
Down by the water's edge, near the culvert drains. There's a path down from behind the blue and gray brick appartments on the corner. Access: Public.

All over CU campus. Actually a dogwood rather than a cherry, you can't eat the fruit straight off the tree (too bitter), but they make unbelievable jelly or syrup (http://www.superluminal.com/cookbook/pudding_sour_cherry_jelly.html). Falling Fruit season: Augu
No fewer than five trees, different varieties.
